title: Vitalé wellness Reel: cut a captured talking-head into platform-ready 9:16 Reel, 9:16 Short and 1:1 feed cuts — quick-cut highlight, multi-platform resize

source.reference: Engaging Video Editor for GEN Checkup

grounding_note: VERBATIM from the Freelancer.com brief ("Engaging Video Editor for GEN Checkup"): the client is "a Germany-based health, wellness, and genetic testing brand focused on personalized health and DNA-based insights"; content is "educational, engaging, and social-media driven, helping people better understand their health, supplements, and genetics"; they need "Short-form social media videos (Instagram Reels, YouTube Shorts, TikTok-style edits)" and note "experience editing German-speaking content and working with subtitles, pacing, and social-media styl[e]". REASONABLY ENRICHED to make it a runnable Adobe-connector benchmark: the real brief implies open-timeline Premiere/After Effects editing (multi-track compositing, motion graphics, burned-in animated subtitles, semantic script-driven trimming) which is NOT in connector scope. So the task is modeled as the genuinely-doable slice the brief actually rewards: the client supplies one raw single-take talking-head capture (a nutrition-scientist explaining a DNA supplement panel) that is overlong with restarts and dead air, plus a short paper edit / hook script and a brand kit; the Adobe chain then auto-selects a punchy highlight sizzle to a target duration (video_create_quick_cut, which chooses the most visually engaging moments — NOT a semantic/script-driven cut) and resizes/exports the finished cut into the exact 9:16 Reel, 9:16 Short and 1:1 feed dimensions the brief lists. Real company/person replaced with the fictional IP-clean brand "Vitalé Labs" and presenter persona "Dr. Nadia Reuter". No burned-in subtitle rendering, motion-graphic compositing, or word-level semantic trimming is claimed (all out of scope); the pacing intent is honored via a delivered plain-text hook-and-hashtag post-caption note, ready for the client to paste into their own post copy.
one_line_ask: Take Vitalé Labs' raw single-take wellness talking-head, auto-cut it into a punchy highlight sizzle at a target duration, then export platform-ready 9:16 Reel, 9:16 Short and 1:1 feed versions plus a hook-and-hashtag post-caption note.
full_brief: Vitalé Labs is a direct-to-consumer wellness and DNA-supplement brand that publishes short educational clips explaining how personalized nutrition works. We just filmed our lead nutrition scientist, Dr. Nadia Reuter, doing a single continuous take (about 3.5 minutes, shot 3840x2160 landscape on a mirrorless camera) explaining our new "GenoBalance" DNA supplement panel. It is a rough take: she rambles, restarts a couple of sentences, and there are long pauses while she checks her notes, shot in a plain untreated office. We need this turned into ONE tight, premium, trustworthy sizzle Reel — clean and modern and educational, NOT flashy or meme-style. Deliverables and specs: (1) cut the rambling take down to a punchy ~35-45 second highlight version that keeps the strongest, most watchable moments and drops the dead air — keep viewers glued from the first second; (2) from that finished cut, export three platform-ready versions at the correct dimensions with no stretching: a 9:16 vertical Instagram Reel (1080x1920), a 9:16 vertical YouTube Short (1080x1920), and a 1:1 square feed post (1080x1080); (3) also hand back a plain-text post-caption note — the 3-second hook line and hashtag set from our attached script — that our team will paste into each platform's post caption. Keep the brand feel restrained and professional per our kit. Attached: the raw take, a one-page paper-edit / hook script marking the lines we care about, and our brand kit.

inputs.name: vitale_genobalance_raw_take.mp4
inputs.gen_prompt: A 3.5-minute single continuous static-tripod talking-head clip, 3840x2160 landscape, of a calm professional woman in her early 40s (fictional nutrition scientist 'Dr. Nadia Reuter') in a lab coat over a knit top, sitting at a light-wood desk in an untreated modern office with a slightly out-of-focus shelf of supplement bottles behind her. She explains a DNA supplement panel to camera, conversationally: she restarts one or two sentences, pauses for a few seconds mid-take to glance at her notes, and meanders. Realistic imperfect handheld-YouTube feel: decent but not studio lighting, natural skin, no captions, no lower-thirds, no on-screen graphics.
inputs.realism_notes: A genuinely raw single-take capture: overlong, with restarts and dead air — exactly the material that justifies a quick-cut + resize pipeline. veo-3 for a realistic long talking-head.

inputs.name: genobalance_paper_edit_hook_script.pdf
inputs.gen_prompt: Generate a one-page paper-edit / hook script PDF for a wellness Reel. Include: a 3-second opening HOOK line ('Your vitamins might be working against your DNA — here's why.'); a bulleted list of the 4-5 lines (with rough in/out timecodes like 00:24-00:33) the client considers the strongest on-message sentences from a talking-head about a DNA supplement panel; a note that restarts, filler and pauses are throwaway; target length 35-45 seconds; a short hashtag set (#DNAHealth #PersonalizedNutrition #Wellness #Supplements). Restrained, professional tone, no medical claims of cure.
inputs.realism_notes: The client's real hand-off: a paper edit and the hook, mirroring the brief's 'pacing/storytelling/retention' ask. Used as reference context for the target duration and the delivered post-caption note (NOT to drive a semantic cut — the connector's quick-cut is visual-engagement-based). PDF like a real client attachment.

inputs.name: vitale_brand_kit.pdf
inputs.gen_prompt: Generate a compact one-page brand-kit PDF for a fictional wellness/DNA-supplement brand 'Vitalé Labs': primary colors (deep teal #0F5C5B, warm sand #E9DCC7, ink #1B1B1B), a clean humanist sans as the wordmark, tone words 'clean, modern, educational, trustworthy, not flashy', social handle @vitalelabs, and note that Reels must be 1080x1920, Shorts 1080x1920, feed posts 1080x1080. No stock logos or real trademarks.
inputs.realism_notes: Provides the brand constraints and the exact export dimensions referenced in the brief, IP-clean and fictional.

outputs.name: vitale_genobalance_reel_1080x1920.mp4
outputs.spec: 9:16 vertical Instagram Reel, 1080x1920, ~35-45s, built from the quick-cut highlight edit; no stretching (source reframed/resized to vertical).

outputs.name: vitale_genobalance_short_1080x1920.mp4
outputs.spec: 9:16 vertical YouTube Short, 1080x1920, same finished cut, exported at Short-correct dimensions.

outputs.name: vitale_genobalance_feed_1080x1080.mp4
outputs.spec: 1:1 square feed post, 1080x1080, same finished cut, resized for in-feed autoplay.

outputs.name: genobalance_highlight_master.mp4
outputs.spec: The intermediate landscape highlight master: the raw take auto-cut down to the ~40s on-message sizzle by visual-engagement selection (source of all three platform resizes).

outputs.name: genobalance_caption_hook_note.txt
outputs.spec: Plain-text post-caption note: the 3-second hook line and the hashtag set drawn from the client's paper-edit / hook script — ready for the team to paste into the Instagram, Short and feed post captions.

connector_workflow.note: Initialize an upload session for the raw take plus the paper-edit script and brand kit so the editing tools can reference them.
Finalize the upload; returns asset URNs for the raw take and the two reference PDFs.
Create a tidy Vitalé/GenoBalance project folder to hold the raw take, intermediates and the three platform exports.
Preview the raw take to confirm it decoded (duration/resolution) before running the cut.

Auto-generate the highlight sizzle: quick-cut selects the most visually engaging moments of the raw take down to a ~35-45s target duration (user_prompt = premium educational wellness sizzle, restrained/not-flashy). Visual-engagement metrics only — no content-aware/semantic analysis. Produces the landscape highlight master (async).
Preview the highlight master to confirm length and framing before resizing.
Resize the finished highlight master to a 9:16 vertical 1080x1920 Instagram Reel (subject-aware reframe, no stretch).
Resize the same master to a 9:16 vertical 1080x1920 YouTube Short.
Resize the same master to a 1:1 square 1080x1080 in-feed post.
Preview the three platform exports to confirm framing/dimensions are correct and nothing is stretched.
Emit the plain-text post-caption note: the 3-second hook line + hashtag set (from the paper-edit / hook script), for the client to paste into each platform's post caption.
Collect the highlight master and the three platform exports into the project delivery folder for hand-off.

chaining_note: Strict output→input chaining throughout. The raw take + two reference PDFs seed the upload session(1)→finalize(2), whose asset URNs feed folder creation(3) and the raw-take preview(4). The uploaded raw take then drives video_create_quick_cut(5), which consumes the take asset and a target duration to produce genobalance_highlight_master.mp4; that master is confirmed by preview(6). The single master fans out into three parallel video_resize calls (7 Reel, 8 Short, 9 feed), each consuming only the master; all three then converge into a single preview(10) and into the delivery copy(12). The post-caption note(11) consumes the supplied paper-edit / hook script. Nothing after the raw input load originates outside the chain — every export descends from the quick-cut master, and the finished platform videos emerge only from the assembled chain.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon video task: 12 connector calls, 7 distinct Adobe tools — above the >=10 calls / >=6 distinct-tool floor for a motion benchmark. Difficulty comes from a genuine multi-stage async media pipeline (video_create_quick_cut [A] → three video_resize [A] exports), where the quick-cut must land a ~35-45s highlight at a controlled target duration and three platform deliverables must land at exact non-stretched dimensions (1080x1920 Reel, 1080x1920 Short, 1080x1080 feed). It respects every hard limit: no generative video, no from-scratch motion graphics, no burned-in subtitle rendering, no multi-track compositing, and NO semantic script-driven trimming (video_create_quick_cut is explicitly visual-engagement-based, not content-aware — the spec uses it only for visual highlight selection, and the KEEP-line/pacing intent is honored solely as a delivered plain-text post-caption note via local_write_recipe_note). video_resize does same-source reframing only, no upscaling beyond source (3840x2160 source downsized to 1080-tall targets); media tools INSPECT/cut existing footage rather than synthesize new content.

reverify.banned_capability_check: None of the banned capabilities are used. No image_crop_and_resize / reframe-crop; no generative fill of new content; no text-to-image or from-scratch art/logo/motion-graphics; no upscaling / super-resolution (the 3840x2160 source is downsized to 1080-tall vertical and 1080 square targets, never up); no object-removal-with-reconstruction / inpainting; no bespoke font/letterform design; no unreachable external account (all steps run on supplied assets via the Adobe connector plus one local write). Critically, no content-aware/script-driven video trimming is claimed: video_create_quick_cut is used strictly for visual-engagement highlight selection to a target duration, matching the tool's actual capability, and the semantic KEEP-line/pacing intent is delivered only as a plain-text post-caption note.
reverify.notes: Step-by-step in-bounds justification: video_create_quick_cut(5) SELECTS and trims existing footage down to a visual highlight — no new frames synthesized, no semantic analysis. video_resize(7-9) does same-source reframe/downscale to exact dimensions with no stretch and no upscaling above source. Burned-in captions and motion-graphic compositing are explicitly NOT claimed as connector outputs — the pacing intent is delivered as a plain-text hook-and-hashtag post-caption note via local_write_recipe_note(11, [L]) for the client's own post copy. asset_* steps are storage/preview/copy only. All async video steps correctly tagged [A]; the single local step tagged [L]. tool_call_count=12 equals the number of connector_workflow steps. distinct_adobe_tools=7 (asset_initialize_file_upload, asset_finalize_file_upload, asset_create_folders, asset_preview_file, asset_copy_assets, video_create_quick_cut, video_resize).
